heading: Officers not to be retired for misconduct
label: 8329 Officers not to be retired for misconduct
text: No officer of the Navy or the Marine Corps may be retired because of misconduct for which trial by court-martial would be appropriate.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)6329.34 U.S.C. 385R.S. 1456.(1st sentence).34 U.S.C. 626–1(a)(1st sentence), ; , .Aug. 7, 1947, ch. 512, § 314(a)61 Stat. 863May 5, 1954, ch. 180, § 20568 Stat. 68The words “for which trial by court-martial would be appropriate” are substituted for the words “but he shall be brought to trial by court-martial for such misconduct”. The peremptory command in the source text is at variance with the theory of the Uniform Code of Military Justice and conflicts with the provisions of articles 30, 32, and 34. The substituted words are in accord with the interpretation placed on R.S. 1456 in v. , 263 U.S. 29, 36 ().DenbyBerryNov. 12, 1923
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 6329 of this title

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019
